based_on: origin/main (9320d51) — the branch that actually carries the Build-RAG base (extractConceptsWithStatus, m8_ingest_checkpoints). Local `main` (268c0f9) is behind origin and lacks them.
model: Opus 4.8 (spec header said Sonnet 4.6; built under Muhammad's direct instruction)

pre_existing_failures_NOT_caused_by_this.root_cause: Both tests read api/ingest-full.js and api/ingest-extract-existing.js, which no longer exist on origin/main — the handlers were refactored to lib/handlers/ingest-full.js and lib/handlers/ingest-extract-existing.js, and these two tests were never re-pathed.
pre_existing_failures_NOT_caused_by_this.proof: Re-ran both with my lib/knowledge-intake.js edit git-stashed (pristine origin/main): identical 14/31 and 48/6. Neither test reads lib/knowledge-intake.js, so this build cannot affect them.
pre_existing_failures_NOT_caused_by_this.suggested_followup: Re-path B101/B102 from api/ to lib/handlers/ (separate cleanup; out of scope here).

schema_check.real_write_target: m8_graph_nodes — has `kind` (claim|entity via JS VALID_KIND), `metadata` jsonb, `extraction_confidence`; NO `type`/`node_type` column
schema_check.decision: schema-free. General `type` (fact|event|person|concept|place|date|ruling) is used ONLY to derive the JS-valid kind (person|place|concept -> entity; everything else -> claim), then dropped. No migration.

changes: lib/knowledge-intake.js — added GENERAL_EXTRACTION_SYSTEM prompt (facts/events/people/places/dates/rulings/concepts; Islamic/historical priority)
lib/knowledge-intake.js — added pure helpers: selectExtractionSystem(mode), buildGeneralExtractionPrompt(), generalTypeToKind(type), deriveExtractionMode(cls, explicitMode)

lib/knowledge-intake.js — parseExtractionOutput(raw, source_class, source_doc_id, mode='math'): NEW 'general' branch normalizes {label,content,type?} into the SAME candidate shape the math path emits (node_type in claim|entity, confidence defaults 'high'); math branch byte-unchanged
lib/knowledge-intake.js — extractConcepts(source_id, mode='general') + extractConceptsWithStatus(source_id, mode='general'): select system prompt + user prompt + parser by mode

lib/knowledge-intake.js — ingestBookText(): new extraction_mode param; derives mode once via deriveExtractionMode(cls, extraction_mode) and threads it to every chapter's extractConceptsWithStatus call

lib/knowledge-intake.js — parseBookIngestMessage(): parses optional `extraction_mode=math` / `mode=math` (comma-delimited; existing grab() lookahead already delimits, so the key list was left untouched to avoid an edge-case title-swallow), returns extraction_mode
lib/knowledge-intake.js — module.exports: added the 5 new pure helpers for testability
lib/orchestrator.js:1436 — forwards `extraction_mode: meta.extraction_mode` into ingestBookText() so the chat math override flows end-to-end (added on explicit instruction)
tests/B-gen-extract-verify.ps1 — NEW PS 5.1 mirror, 66 checks across all 7 spec criteria
spec_deviations_and_why: Spec said the math parser 'only returns {label, content}else' — WRONG: it requires {node_type in claim|entity, label, content, confidence in high|medium|low}. The general prompt emits NEITHER node_type NOR confidence, so the existing strict parser would have silently dropped every general item. Fix: the general branch synthesizes node_type (from type) and confidence (default high) so nodes actually reach m8_graph_nodes — this is the real unblock the spec was reaching for.
Spec named table m8_knowledge_nodes; it does not exist. Real table m8_graph_nodes has no type column. Followed the spec's 'no column -> drop the field' branch.

Spec referenced parseBookIngestCommand()/handleBookIngestCommand(); actual names are parseBookIngestMessage() (in knowledge-intake.js) and inline handling in orchestrator.js. Implemented against the real names.

extractConcepts default mode is 'general' per spec. This also routes the existing document paths (converter.js, lib/handlers/ingest-full.js, lib/handlers/ingest-extract-existing.js, orchestrator re-extract) through general extraction — exactly the goal (uploaded/established docs now extract). Those candidates still carry extraction_confidence/node_type, so those call sites work unchanged. Forbidden files were NOT edited.

wiring.status: COMPLETE — extraction_mode now flows end-to-end through chat.
wiring.what: lib/orchestrator.js:1436 forwards `extraction_mode: meta.extraction_mode` into the ingestBookText({...}) call. Added on Muhammad's explicit instruction, overriding the spec's do-not-touch list for this one line.
wiring.default_safe: meta.extraction_mode is 'general' unless the user types extraction_mode=math / mode=math. cls in this path is only established|speculative (normalizeSourceClass never yields 'mathematical'), so forwarding the 'general' default produces the same mode the cls-derivation would — zero behavior change for existing book ingests; it only adds the math-override capability.
wiring.regression_check: B102 stayed 48/6 (byte-identical pre/post edit; the 6 fails are the pre-existing missing-api/-file ones), B78b 18/0, B-gen-extract 66/0.

files_changed: lib/orchestrator.js (1-line extraction_mode forward, per explicit instruction)
live_verify_procedure: After Muhammad's explicit deploy: in M8 chat, `ingest this as a book: title=Test Book, source_class=established` with a small (<500-word) non-math excerpt attached. Expect chapters>0 AND nodes>0. Confirm via GET /api/knowledge?fn=inventory (nodes_in_books>0) and ?fn=status. Paid GEMINI_API_KEY confirmed working 2026-06-22 per spec.
deploy: NOT deployed. Branch-only push to origin/feat/general-extraction. Awaiting Muhammad's explicit 'deploy it'.
